meta.notes: Read-only research log. No trades are ever placed by this routine.

positions.risk_flag: OVER_CONCENTRATED - 74.4% of NAV vs 35% trim threshold, WORSE than last run (69.8%) as stock rallied +5.1% since 2026-07-11 with no dry powder to rebalance

catalyst.name: $25B investment-grade bond deal (5 tranches, settled ~Jun 26 2026) + staggered IPO lockup
catalyst.status: No new negative credit/bond news found. First lockup tranche (20%) still expected 2nd full trading day after Q2 2026 earnings (~$123B in shares per public estimates), pegged late Jul/early Aug 2026; conditional bonus 10% release requires 5-of-10 closes >= $175.50 (30% above $135 IPO) before that print - stock at $125.88 is well below that bar so bonus tranche will NOT trigger; further 7% tranches Aug-Oct; full 180-day lockup expires Dec 8 2026; Musk's ~6.4B shares locked ~366 days separately
catalyst.next_check: Q2 2026 earnings date announcement; stock reaction into lockup; confirm bonus-release threshold stays unmet

positions.risk_flag: WATCH - within 35% cap and NAV% shrank vs last run only because the position lost value (-13.7% since 2026-07-11), not because of trimming

flags: SLS concentration worsened to 74.4% of NAV (from 69.8%) purely on price appreciation - no thesis-breaking news, no fresh catalyst confirmation, no 80th-event announcement found in this run's search.

SPCX down 13.7% since the 2026-07-11 run with no negative company-specific news identified - looks broad/technical, not catalyst-driven; but it also confirms the stock is far below the $175.50 bonus-unlock trigger price, so only the base 20% tranche is in play post-Q2-earnings.
Zero USD cash buffer remains - account fully invested, no dry powder to rebalance SLS down to target even if desired (not on margin, no forced-liquidation risk).
No trades were placed. This routine is read-only.
